areas_remaining: xsim regression scope and pass bar

Vivado target contract.question: What naming convention should the Vivado xsim certify target use?
Vivado target contract.answer: Use the same style as the Quartus registry names and the current Vivado flake target-profile vocabulary. The working target should be versioned and profile-aware, with planner discretion to settle the exact literal name after re-reading the current Vivado flake. Current preferred shape: vivado-2025-2-1-synth-sim-full-xsim while only synth-sim-full works; a later sim-only target should be named distinctly rather than silently changing the profile behind the same target name.

Vivado target contract.question: Which Vivado package profile should Phase 4 consume?
Vivado target contract.answer: Consume synth-sim-full for now because the Vivado flake is still under development in another session and synth-sim-full is the only working profile at this point. Do not assume sim-only is usable until the Vivado flake proves it.
options_presented: start with vivado-sim, allow fallback to full

Vivado target contract.question: How should stub-vs-real Vivado be gated?
Vivado target contract.answer: Hard gate on lib.${system}.isStub == false before exposing/running the target. Phase 4 sign-off must fail loudly if the Vivado input regresses to a stub.
options_presented: Allow stub target to evaluate but fail at runtime
Eval can include target, certify run refuses stub

Vivado target contract.question: How should the Vivado flake input be referenced and pinned?
Vivado target contract.answer: Use a git+ssh input pointing at the qualified Vivado repo and take the current flake. Because another Codex session is actively updating that flake, Phase 4 research/planning/execution must periodically re-check the Vivado flake, especially flake.nix and the latest commit, and pin the SVUnit flake.lock to the current accepted Vivado revision before sign-off.
options_presented: Direct flake input to local qualified Vivado repo path and lock it
Path reference without lock update until run works
Defer exact pin choice but require final lock before sign-off
